Xbox Content Signing Options

By Pete Isensee, Development Lead, Xbox Advanced Technology Group

In order to protect content on the hard disk and memory units, the Xbox® video game system from Microsoft® provides a variety of signing techniques.

This white paper lists the various available options and describes when and how to use each method. It also includes detailed information on how various signatures are calculated. For information on testing content signatures, refer to the Content Protection on Xbox white paper.

By Data Region

Each data region on an Xbox console is designed to store different types of information. The regions have different requirements when it comes to security.

User Region

User data regions are locations where players can store saved games, profiles, and other user-generated data. Xbox Memory Units are always user data regions. The U: partition on the Xbox hard disk is also a user data region. Technical Certification Requirements (TCRs) state that all content written to user data regions must be signed. User data can be signed as roamable (usable on other consoles), non-roamable (locked to one console) or Live (requires an Xbox Live™ connection). User data must be signed and verified using the XCalculateSignature family of functions.

Persistent Data Region

The Xbox persistent data region is the T: hard disk partition, dedicated to storing generic, non-user-specific data, including things such as high-score tables, new levels, custom maps, or other game content. Content downloaded from Xbox Live is stored on the T: drive. TCRs mandate that all content written to persistent data regions be signed as non-roamable (locked to one console). Content downloaded from Xbox Live is automatically signed during the XOnlineContentInstall process and must be verified using XLocateSignatureByName/Index along with XCalculateContentSignature. Content created by the game must be signed using the XCalculateSignature family of functions and the CONTENT flag. Content that could conceivably be shared online with other consoles or uploaded to Xbox Live must be signed using XCalculateSignature with the flag ONLINE. Shared online content must be verified using XOnlineVerifySignature.

Utility Data Region

The Xbox utility data region is the Z: hard disk partition. It is designed as a fast cache or scratch area, useful for storing audio assets or level data. Data in the utility region is not required to be signed. Developers are encouraged to validate content on the utility region when it makes sense. For instance, it's rarely useful to sign texture data or raw audio. The performance overhead is too high, and even if the data is corrupted the worse outcome is a video or audio glitch. However, it is important to validate file headers and other content when corrupt content could allow exploitation. For instance, file manifests, configuration files, AI scripts, and world state data should be signed. The XCalculateSignature family of functions can be used to sign this data. Use the NON_ROAMABLE flag.

Xbox Storage Signature Options

Determining the best way of signing content is a product of both security and flexibility. For instance, signing saved games with Xbox Live keys is extremely secure, but those save games can be read and written only while the player is online. The following tables show the various options available for protecting data.

In these tables, low security means that modified data can be easily re-signed by a dedicated attacker, transferred to another console, and still validate properly. High security means that even though data can be easily re-signed, it will not validate on another console. Highest security means that it is quite difficult to re-sign modified data.

By Xbox Data Region

Partition Signature required Signature options Security Xbox Live sign-in required
F–M, U (user data) Yes Roamable Low No
Non-roamable High No
Xbox Live Highest Yes
T (persistent) Yes Non-roamable High No
Xbox Live Highest Yes
Z (utility) No No signature None No
Non-roamable High No

By Content Type

Content Signature required Signature options Security Xbox Live sign-in required
Save games, profiles, user data Yes Roamable Low No
Non-roamable High No
Xbox Live Highest Yes
Downloads, high scores Yes Non-roamable High No
Xbox Live Highest Yes
Cached data No No signature None No
Non-roamable High No
Xbox Live uploads, shared online data Yes Xbox Live Highest Yes

By Security Level

Security Signature options Xbox Live sign-in required
None No signature No
Low Roamable No
High Non-roamable No
Highest Xbox Live Yes

By Function and Flag

Function XCALCSIG Flag Content
XCalculateContentSignature N/A Persistent data
XCalculateSignatureBegin SAVE_GAME or 0 Roamable user data
XCalculateSignatureBegin NON_ROAMABLE Non-roamable user data
XCalculateSignatureBegin CONTENT Persistent data
XCalculateSignatureBegin ONLINE Shared online data
XCalculateSignatureBeginEx SAVE_GAME or 0 Roamable user data for alternate titles
XCalculateSignatureBeginEx NON_ROAMABLE Non-roamable user data or persistent data for alternate titles
XInstallContentSignatures N/A Persistent data installed via game disc
XLoadContentSignatures N/A Persistent data
XLoadContentSignaturesEx N/A Persistent data for alternate titles
XLocateSignatureByName N/A Persistent data
XLocateSignatureByIndex N/A Persistent data
XOnlineSignatureVerify N/A Shared online data
  • User data includes save games, profiles, etc.
  • Persistent data includes content installed via XInstallContentSignatures content downloaded via XOnlineInstallContent or content on the persistent data region signed with the CONTENT flag.
  • Shared online data includes future online uploads (such as game clips) as well as content intended to be shared online with other consoles.

Xbox Live Signatures

Xbox Live signatures provide the highest security level. They are designed for hard disk content that will be uploaded to the Xbox Live service or transmitted to another Xbox console's hard disk. The reason secure signatures are so important in these scenarios is that shared content introduces a high risk to the Xbox platform. A hacker could introduce modified content that could potentially be distributed to thousands of consoles. Truly malicious hackers could render multiple consoles unusable.

Any content read or written to the Xbox hard disk that could ever be transmitted and stored on either the Xbox Live service or another Xbox console should be Xbox Live signed. The signature must be recomputed whenever the content is modified and rewritten. A single failure to sign or verify content introduces a security hole. Live signatures require that players be connected to Live whenever the content is read or written to the hard disk. Content that has been created or modified while the player was offline cannot be safely shared, because an attacker could modify the content without detection. Similarly, content that has been transmitted using system link play can also not be safely shared for the same reason. In general, the only time the console is considered secure is when the console is signed into Xbox Live.

Because of these complexities, it's important to design the game so that potentially shared content can be created and modified only while a player is online. The actual signing process is similar to offline signing. Use the XCalculateSignature ONLINE flag over all files that may be shared. Store the signature on disk along with the data. Live signatures contain more information than just cryptographic bits. They also contain data about the game, Xbox, time, and signed-in users. This information allows content to be revoked at a high level of granularity.

The Xbox Live verification process is completely different than offline verification. At verification time, the digest of the content is recomputed using the DIGEST flag. The original signature is read from storage. Both the digest and the original signature are sent to the Live service for validation. The relevant verification functions are XOnlineVerifySignature and XOnlineVerifySignatureGetResults. The Live service evaluates the incoming digest and original signature to determine whether the content is valid. The service also evaluates whether the signature should be revoked based on the title, Xbox, or player(s) who signed the content.

Creating Xbox Live signatures and verifying them requires that the player be signed in to Xbox Live. Signatures cannot be created or verified without a Live connection.

By default, Live signatures are roamable. Data signed on one console can be transmitted, stored, and verified on another console. Non-roamable Live signatures can be created by computing a signature using the NON_ROAMABLE flag, then signing that signature with the ONLINE flag. At verification time, re-compute the NON_ROAMABLE signature, and send the DIGEST of that signature along with the saved Live signature for validation. Because the non-roamable signature is different on every console, only content that was signed by the same console will validate properly.

Keys

Secure signing involves the use of cryptographic keys. See the Xbox Security Architecture white paper for additional information. The XCalculateSignatureBegin flags indicate what type of key (or keys) will be used during the signing process. The following table shows what keys are used based on the signing function and input flag.

Function XCALCSIG_FLAG_... Key data
XCalculateSignatureBegin 0 or SAVE_GAME Per-title key
XCalculateSignatureBegin NON_ROAMABLE Per-title key + Xbox key
XCalculateSignatureBegin CONTENT None
XCalculateSignatureBegin DIGEST None
XCalculateSignatureBegin ONLINE Xbox Live key
XCalculateSignatureBeginEx 0 or SAVE_GAME Alternate title key
XCalculateSignatureBeginEx NON_ROAMABLE Alternate title key + Xbox key
XCalculateContentSignature N/A None
XLoadContentSignatures N/A Per-title key + Xbox key
XLoadContentSignaturesEx N/A Alternate title key + Xbox key

The SAVE_GAME flag computes a hash using the following formula:

    Signature = HMAC SHA-1( Per-title key, Data )

The NON_ROAMABLE flag computes a hash using:

    Signature = HMAC SHA-1( Per-title key + Xbox key, Data )

The DIGEST flag computes a hash without any key data. A digest computed by title A on one Xbox will exactly match a digest computed by title B on a different Xbox. Digests are not by themselves secure. Digests are generated using the following formula:

    Signature = Digest = SHA-1( Data )

Interestingly, the CONTENT flag gives the same results as the DIGEST flag. Content package signatures are in fact a combination of digests and signatures. For maximum performance, content packages contain a list of digests (often one per file, although files may have multiple digests to facilitate loading smaller blocks of the file quickly), and the digests themselves are signed with per-title and Xbox keys, just like standard non-roamable content. The integrity of content packages is checked using XLoadContentSignatures, which validates the digests using XCalculateSignatureBegin(NON_ROAMABLE), and XCalculateContentSignature, which validates a piece of content against an already valid digest using XCalculateSignatureBegin(DIGEST). The XLocateSignatureByName/Index functions locate a specific digest from the list.

The Xbox Live key used by the ONLINE flag is generated by the Xbox Live service during the XOnlineLogon process. Every time a player (or players) sign in, the Signature Service generates a new key, using the following formula:

    XboxConfigInfo = TitleID + XboxID + XUID(s) of players + FILETIME
    LiveKey = HMAC SHA-1( LiveMasterKey, XboxConfigInfo )

The XboxConfigInfo is sent to the Xbox Live service at logon, which "signs" the configuration data using a master key it has stored. The resulting signature becomes the LiveKey used by the console for the online session. The master key is never transmitted. The ONLINE flag performs the following calculation:

    Digest = SHA-1( Data )
    LiveSig = HMAC SHA-1( LiveKey, Digest )

The full Xbox Live signature is a binary block of data (BLOB) that consists of the LiveSig plus XboxConfigInfo. The title stores this signature BLOB on the HD or MU. Then, at XOnlineSignatureVerify time, the client sends a newly computed digest along with the stored signature BLOB. The Xbox Live service then recomputes the LiveKey and the signature, and compares the computed signature with the one received by the client.

    Xbox Live key = HMAC SHA-1( LiveMasterKey, XboxConfigInfo )
    Signature = HMAC SHA-1( LiveKey, Digest )

If the signatures match, then the content has not been tampered with. The Xbox Live service can also fail the verification if the XboxConfigInfo identifies a banned player or banned console.

Performance

The XCalculateSignature functions can process approximately 35 MB/sec. Performance is gated on the speed of the SHA-1 calculation, which has been optimized and tuned for Xbox (Pentium III).

Code Snippets

The following code snippets show how to sign and verify signatures for various types of content.

Building Signatures Piecemeal

Signatures are created using three functions that all start with XCalculateSignature and end with Begin (or BeginEx), Update, or End. This set of functions allows signatures to be created without requiring the entire data block to be in memory simultaneously. It also allows signatures to be generated when files are streamed in from disk.

    // Begin signature calculation
    HANDLE hSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_WHATEVER );
    for( ... ) // for each file chunk
    {
        // Read and hash the chunk of data
        ReadFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwRead, NULL );
        XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hSig, pData, dwDataSize );
    }
    // Extract the final signature
    VOID* pSig = _alloca( XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_WHATEVER ) );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hSig, pSig );

Saved Games

Save games, profiles, and other user data are typically signed with the SAVE_GAME flag, which specifies that the data is roamable. They may also be signed with the NON_ROAMABLE flag. Here's how to sign a save game:

    // Signature is only a few dozen bytes so allocated on stack
    DWORD dwSigType = XCALCSIG_FLAG_SAVE_GAME; // or XCALCSIG_FLAG_NON_ROAMABLE
    DWORD dwSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( dwSigType );
    VOID* pSig = _alloca( dwSigSize );

    // Calculate signature of the data
    HANDLE hSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( dwSigType );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hSig, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hSig, pSig );

    // Write the signature and the data to disk or MU
    WriteFile( hFile, pSig, dwSigSize, &dwWritten, NULL );
    WriteFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwWritten, NULL );

To verify the save, follow the same steps and compare signatures:

    // Read the signature and the data from disk or MU
    DWORD dwSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( dwSigType );
    VOID* pSigFromFile = _alloca( dwSigSize );
    ReadFile( hFile, pSigFromFile, dwSigSize, &dwRead, NULL );
    ReadFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwRead, NULL );

    // Re-compute signature
    VOID* pSig = _alloca( dwSigSize );
    HANDLE hSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( dwSigType );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hSig, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hSig, pSig );

    // Compare signatures
    if( memcmp( pSigFromFile, pSig, dwSigSize ) != 0 )
        ; // data is invalid

Content Packages Downloaded from Xbox Live

Content packages installed by Downloader.XBE are automatically signed. If your game downloads content using XOnlineContentInstall directly, the content is also automatically signed:

    // Download, unpack, and sign the content as non-roamable
    HRESULT hr = XOnlineContentInstall( OfferingID, NULL, &hInstall );
    do
        hr = XOnlineTaskContinue( hInstall );
    while( hr == XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING );
    XOnlineTaskClose( hInstall );

Whether Downloader is used or not, games must include the proper verification code for content packages:

    // Enumerate content packages; in this case just get the first one
    HANDLE hFind = XFindFirstContent( "T:\\", 0xFFFFFFFF, &FindData );
    XFindClose( hFind );

    // Load signatures of selected package into memory
    HANDLE hSig = XLoadContentSignatures( FindData.szContentDirectory );
    if( hSig == NULL )
        ; // invalid content
    
    // Find the signature for the file (or file block) that's being loaded
    BYTE* pSigFromFile;
    XLocateSignatureByName( hSig, strFile, dwOffset, dwDataSize,
                            &pSigFromFile, &dwSigSize );

    // Read the data from disk
    HANDLE hFile = CreateFile( strFile, ... );
    SetFilePointer( hFile, dwOffset, NULL, FILE_BEGIN );
    ReadFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwRead, NULL );

    // Re-compute signature
    BYTE* pSig = (BYTE*)_alloca( dwSigSize );
    XCalculateContentSignature( pData, dwDataSize, pSig, &dwSigSize );

    // Compare signatures
    if( memcmp( pSigFromFile, pSig, dwSigSize ) != 0 )
        ; // data is invalid

    // Free signature resources
    XCloseContentSignatures( hSig );

Note that XCalculateContentSignature is implemented in terms of the XCalculateSignature. It's equivalent to the following:

    *pdwSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_CONTENT );
    HANDLE hSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_CONTENT );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hSig, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hSig, pSig );

Shared Content Copied Between Consoles

Content that's stored on one Xbox console and then transmitted and stored on another Xbox console using Xbox Live must be signed with Xbox Live signatures.

    // Request signature service at sign-in
    DWORD Services[] = { XONLINE_SIGNATURE_SERVICE };
    XOnlineLogon( NULL, Services, 1, NULL, &hLogon );

    // Signature is only 100 bytes so allocated on stack
    DWORD dwLiveSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_ONLINE );
    VOID* pLiveSig = _alloca( dwLiveSigSize );

    // Calculate the Xbox Live signature; requires a valid Xbox Live connection
    HANDLE hSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_ONLINE );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hSig, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hSig, pLiveSig );

    // Write the signature and the data to disk or MU
    WriteFile( hFile, pLiveSig, dwLiveSigSize, &dwWritten, NULL );
    WriteFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwWritten, NULL );

Both the data and signature must be sent to the receiver.

    // Send both the signature and the data to another console
    send( sock, (const char*)pLiveSig, dwLiveSigSize, 0 );
    send( sock, (const char*)pData, dwDataSize, 0 );

    // On other console, write the signature and data to disk or MU
    WriteFile( hFile, pLiveSig, dwLiveSigSize, &dwWritten, NULL );
    WriteFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwWritten, NULL );

Verifying the signature requires recalculating the digest based on the data, then sending the digest and the stored signature to Xbox Live for validation.

    // Request signature service at sign in
    DWORD Services[] = { XONLINE_SIGNATURE_SERVICE };
    XOnlineLogon( NULL, Services, 1, NULL, &hLogon );

    // Read the Xbox Live signature and the data from disk or MU
    DWORD dwLiveSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_ONLINE );
    BYTE* pLiveSigFromFile = (BYTE*)_alloca( dwLiveSigSize );
    ReadFile( hFile, pLiveSigFromFile, dwLiveSigSize, &dwRead, NULL );
    ReadFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwRead, NULL );

    // Calculate the digest of the data
    DWORD dwDigestSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_DIGEST );
    BYTE* pDigest = (BYTE*)_alloca( dwDigestSize );
    HANDLE hDigest = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_DIGEST );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hDigest, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hDigest, pDigest );

    // Prepare to verify the Xbox Live signature
    XONLINE_SIGNATURE_TO_VERIFY SigVerify;
    SigVerify.cbDigest = dwDigestSize;
    SigVerify.pbDigest = pDigest;
    SigVerify.cbOnlineSignature = dwLiveSigSize;
    SigVerify.pbOnlineSignature = pLiveSigFromFile;

    // Contact the Xbox Live service to verify the signature
    HRESULT hr = XOnlineSignatureVerify( &SigVerify, 1, NULL, &hVerify );
    do
        hr = XOnlineTaskContinue( hVerify );
    while( hr == XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING );

    HRESULT* pResult;
    XOnlineSignatureVerifyGetResults( hVerify, &pResult, &dwResults );
    if( FAILED( pResult[0] ) )
        ; // data is invalid or has been revoked
    
    XOnlineTaskClose( hVerify );

Shared Content Used Both Offline and Online

Content used in offline modes (single player, system link, etc.) that is also transferred and stored online must have both an Xbox Live signature and a non–Xbox Live signature. In offline modes, the content can be validated with the offline signature. The content must only be created and modified while online.

    // Request signature service at sign-in
    DWORD Services[] = { XONLINE_SIGNATURE_SERVICE };
    XOnlineLogon( NULL, Services, 1, NULL, &hLogon );

    // Allocate space on the stack for the signatures. Content can be offline signed
    // as roamable or non-roamable, depending on the intended usage. Non-roamable
    // content must be resigned when it is transferred.
    DWORD dwOfflineSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_NON_ROAMABLE );
    DWORD dwLiveSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_ONLINE );
    BYTE* pOfflineSig = (BYTE*)_alloca( dwOfflineSigSize );
    VOID* pLiveSig = _alloca( dwLiveSigSize );

    // Calculate offline and Xbox Live signatures of the data
    HANDLE hOfflineSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_NON_ROAMABLE );
    HANDLE hLiveSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_ONLINE );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hOfflineSig, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hLiveSig, pData, dwDataSize);
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hOfflineSig, pOfflineSig );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hLiveSig, pLiveSig );

    // Write the signatures and the data to disk or MU
    WriteFile( hFile, pOfflineSig, dwOfflineSigSize, &dwWritten, NULL );
    WriteFile( hFile, pLiveSig, dwLiveSigSize, &dwWritten, NULL );
    WriteFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwWritten, NULL );

To verify for offline read-only usage, validate the offline signature.

    // Read offline signature and data from disk or MU; skip Xbox Live signature
    DWORD dwOfflineSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_NON_ROAMABLE );
    DWORD dwLiveSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_ONLINE );
    BYTE* pOfflineSigFromFile = (BYTE*)_alloca( dwOfflineSigSize );
    ReadFile( hFile, pOfflineSigFromFile, dwOfflineSigSize, &dwRead, NULL );
    SetFilePointer( hFile, dwLiveSigSize, NULL, FILE_CURRENT );
    ReadFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwRead, NULL );

    // Re-compute offline signature
    VOID* pOfflineSig = _alloca( dwOfflineSigSize );
    HANDLE hSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_NON_ROAMABLE );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hSig, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hSig, pOfflineSig );

    // Compare signatures
    if( memcmp( pOfflineSigFromFile, pOfflineSig, dwOfflineSigSize ) != 0 )
        ; // data is invalid

To verify for online usage, validate the Xbox Live signature.

    // Request signature service at sign in
    DWORD Services[] = { XONLINE_SIGNATURE_SERVICE };
    XOnlineLogon( NULL, Services, 1, NULL, &hLogon );

    // Read the Xbox Live signature and the data from disk or MU; skip offline sig
    DWORD dwOfflineSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_NON_ROAMABLE );
    DWORD dwLiveSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_ONLINE );
    BYTE* pLiveSigFromFile = (BYTE*)_alloca( dwLiveSigSize );
    SetFilePointer( hFile, dwOfflineSigSize, NULL, FILE_BEGIN );
    ReadFile( hFile, pLiveSigFromFile, dwLiveSigSize, &dwRead, NULL );
    ReadFile( hFile, pData, dwDataSize, &dwRead, NULL );

    // Calculate the digest of the data
    DWORD dwDigestSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_DIGEST );
    BYTE* pDigest = (BYTE*)_alloca( dwDigestSize );
    HANDLE hDigest = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_DIGEST );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hDigest, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hDigest, pDigest );

    // Prepare to verify the Xbox Live signature
    XONLINE_SIGNATURE_TO_VERIFY SigVerify;
    SigVerify.cbDigest = dwDigestSize;
    SigVerify.pbDigest = pDigest;
    SigVerify.cbOnlineSignature = dwLiveSigSize;
    SigVerify.pbOnlineSignature = pLiveSigFromFile;

    // Contact the Xbox Live service to verify the signature
    HRESULT hr = XOnlineSignatureVerify( &SigVerify, 1, NULL, &hVerify );
    do
        hr = XOnlineTaskContinue( hVerify );
    while( hr == XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING );

    HRESULT* pResult;
    XOnlineSignatureVerifyGetResults( hVerify, &pResult, &dwResults );
    if( FAILED( pResult[0] ) )
        ; // data is invalid or has been revoked

If the content has been transferred to another console, once the Xbox Live signature has been initially verified, the content can be re-signed with a new non-roamable signature that locks the content to the box.

    DWORD dwOfflineSigSize = XCalculateSignatureGetSize( XCALCSIG_FLAG_NON_ROAMABLE );
    BYTE* pOfflineSig = (BYTE*)_alloca( dwOfflineSigSize );

    // Calculate new non-roamable offline signature
    HANDLE hOfflineSig = XCalculateSignatureBegin( XCALCSIG_FLAG_NON_ROAMABLE );
    XCalculateSignatureUpdate( hOfflineSig, pData, dwDataSize );
    XCalculateSignatureEnd( hOfflineSig, pOfflineSig );

    // Update the signature
    WriteFile( hFile, pOfflineSig, dwOfflineSigSize, &dwWritten, NULL );

Game Clips (Statistics Attachments)

The signing of attachments happens automatically during XOnlineStorageUpload.

    // Store statistics
    HRESULT hr = XOnlineStatWrite( 1, &Spec, NULL, &hStatWrite );
    do
        hr = XOnlineTaskContinue( hStatWrite );
    while( hr == XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING );    

    // If attachment allowed, upload it. The content is automatically
    // Xbox Live-signed by the XOnlineStorageUpload task.
    if( hr == XONLINE_S_STAT_CAN_UPLOAD_ATTACHMENT )
    {
        hr = XOnlineStatWriteGetResult( hStatWrite, &hUploadServer, &pRefs, &dwRefs );
        hr = XOnlineStorageUpload( hUploadServer, strDir, 0, NULL, &hUpload );
        do
            hr = XOnlineTaskContinue( hUpload );
        while ( hr == XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING );
        XOnlineTaskClose( hUpload );
    }

    XOnlineTaskClose( hStatWrite );

The verification of attachments happens automatically during XOnlineStorageDownload. The API is somewhat misnamed, because it will access (and verify) cached attachments if they are already on the player's HD.

    // Read statistics
    HRESULT hr = XOnlineStatReadGetResult( hStatRead, 1, &Spec, dwExtra, pExtra );
    XOnlineTaskClose( hStatRead );

    // If attachment found, download it. The content is automatically verified
    // by the XOnlineStorageDownload task.
    if( Spec.pStats[ ID_ATTACHMENT ].type == XONLINE_STAT_LPCWSTR )
    {
        hr = XOnlineStorageDownload( XONLINESTORAGE_FACILITY_STATS, 0, 
                                     Spec.pStats[ ID_ATTACHMENT ].lpString,
                                     NULL, 0, NULL, &hDownload );
        do 
            hr = XOnlineTaskContinue( hDownload );
        while ( hr == XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING );
        XOnlineTaskClose( hDownload );
    }

Best Practices

  • Sign all content on the hard disk and MUs, with the exception of cached audio, video, and texture data on the Z: drive.
  • On the Z: drive, validate file headers, manifests, configuration files, AI scripts, and state data.
  • For maximum security, prefer Xbox Live signatures or non-roamable signatures. Avoid roamable signatures unless the content must truly be roamable.
  • Most signatures are 20 bytes. Xbox Live signatures are 100 bytes because they include additional information. For best performance, allocate signature storage on the stack using _alloca.
  • Xbox Live signatures require an online connection whenever shared content is written or read from storage. Design games that share content online so that potentially shared content can be created and modified only while a player is online.
  • Signing and authenticating data doesn't guarantee that your game is safe from hackers. Hackers have successfully modified and re-signed content without the game detecting it. Follow secure coding guidelines. Assume that all content coming from the hard disk or MUs is bogus, even if the signature is valid. See the Xbox Security Best Practices white paper for more information.
Unpublished work. © 2000 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
